items.title: The debate on ultra-processed foods
items.summary: The strength of the evidence linking ultra-processed foods to adverse health outcomes polarizes the opinion of the scientific community. Two Perspectives published side-by-side in this issue of Nature Food explore these different views and their implications for food policy.

items.title: Publisher Correction: Five archetypes of small-scale fisheries reveal a continuum of production strategies to guide governance and policymaking

items.title: Policy synergies outweigh trade-offs for NH 3 and N 2 O co-control in China
items.summary: Targeted strategies to simultaneously reduce NH3 and N2O emissions in agriculture are urgently needed. By combining high-resolution emissions inventories with policy scenario analysis, this study evaluates China’s national policies for abating Nr emissions and synergies between NH3 and N2O abatem...

items.title: Oil crop supply chains drive rising global biodiversity loss and outsource impacts to the tropics
items.summary: Spatial assessments reveal that oil crop expansion, particularly in tropical regions, threaten global biodiversity. Hotspots of production and consumption are identified, showing that consumption-side interventions can aid in developing strategies to reduce biodiversity impacts.
